title: Today Was the Day Galileo Caved
gist: A historical retrospective on Galileo's June 1633 trial, where the 70-year-old scientist was forced under threat of death to recant his heliocentric views — championed in his 1632 Dialogue — that violated the Church's 1616 edict against challenging geocentrism.

insights.claim: Galileo's 1632 book Dialogue Concerning the Two Chief World Systems, comparing Copernican heliocentrism against Church-backed Aristotelian geocentrism, violated the Church's 1616 edict and triggered his heresy trial.
insights.speaker: Bob Grant
insights.so_what: Historical case of institutional power suppressing science; context only.
insights.quote: the straw that broke the camel’s back was the 70-year-old luminary’s 1632 book Dialogue Concerning the Two Chief World Systems
insights.locator: Among all his insights and discoveries

insights.claim: Forced to recant under threat of death after decades defending heliocentrism with his telescopes, Galileo knelt before his accusers and formally declared his guilt in a written document.
insights.speaker: Bob Grant
insights.so_what: Iconic example of coerced scientific suppression; no market read.
insights.quote: Forced to recant his position under the threat of death, the scientific giant formally declared his guilt
insights.locator: After decades of defending his view
